# Midnight NIGHT Token Debug Report

## Key Findings

Analysis of 49,934 eligible addresses, 49,934 API schedules, and 52,762 on-chain transactions reveals a **single root cause** manifesting as multiple symptoms:

**The Midnight backend fails to recognize successful on-chain thaw transactions.** When a first-thaw claim succeeds on-chain but the backend marks it as `skipped` or `failed`, three things happen:

1. **287 addresses** have thaw #0 marked `skipped` despite all 287 having a successful on-chain redeem transaction
2. **234 addresses** have thaw #1 amounts inflated (>120% of first-thaw delivery) because the backend rolled the "unclaimed" amount forward. **0.000000 excess NIGHT** is promised in inflated schedules
3. **3623 thaws** show as "redeemable" in the API but the NIGHT was already delivered on-chain — users see a claim button for tokens they already received

### Status Breakdown

| Status | Count | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| skipped | 287 | all thaw #0, all have successful on-chain tx |
| failed | 50 | all have tx_id, 50 tx_ids not found on-chain |
| confirming | 2 | stuck in confirming state |

These issues are **not correlated** with shared escrow addresses or dirty funding UTXOs — rates match the baseline population.

## On-Chain Data

| Metric | Value | Detail |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Treasury Transactions | 52,762 | 2,828 non-redeem (setup/admin) |
| Redeem Transactions | 49,934 | all valid contracts |
| Eligible Addresses | 49,934 | 1:1 with redeems (all unique) |
| Total NIGHT Distributed | 2,602,753,844.208125 | valid contracts only |
| Escrow Addresses | 46,904 | 46,904 synced / 1,514 shared (4,544 addrs) |
| Dirty Funding UTXOs | 1,321 | 2.6% of redeems had extra tokens |

## API Schedule Data

| Metric | Value | Detail |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Schedules Fetched | 49,934 | 0 remaining |
| Addresses with Thaws | 49,934 | 0 returned not-eligible |
| Schedule Errors | 0 | excluding not-eligible |
